doctrine_links.reasoning: Interception of the Qatari royal family's (Sheikha Moza) correspondence is political targeting of the UAE's principal GCC rival in the pre-2021 blockade era; the Michelle Obama thread is collateral to surveilling Qatari leadership's external contacts. The op advances the UAE's regional-influence posture against Qatar.

A state-run messaging app harvesting conversations, movements, and relationships of users inside the UAE and abroad is a mass-surveillance instrument serving regime security; it is the population-wide collection layer beneath the doctrine's targeted dissident/journalist surveillance, so no single targeted pillar fits.

Pegasus targeting of the widow of murdered dissident journalist Jamal Khashoggi is surveillance of the exiled-dissident/journalist milieu the regime treats as a security threat; the op advances regime-protection surveillance of diaspora critics rather than any state-collection objective.

State-financed surveillance and smearing of 1,000+ individuals and 400 organisations framed as Muslim Brotherhood affiliates across Europe is transnational repression of the regime's core ideological opponents; it spans diaspora dissidents, journalists and organisations, so no single pillar fits. Overrides the regional-influence hint because that doctrine's pillars (Qatar/Iran/post-Accords) do not match a pan-European MB campaign.

A custom espionage backdoor against a Middle East governmental entity (related sample surfaced from Qatar) is intelligence collection against a regional state, advancing the UAE's regional-posture objectives; the specific target state is unspecified, so no Qatar/Iran/post-Accords pillar can be fixed.

Ghostwriter (Belarusian-government-tied) lures on 'political prisoners' and an 'Anti-Corruption Initiative' aimed at Belarusian opposition activists are surveillance of the domestic opposition, directly advancing the doctrine's regime-stability and opposition-monitoring objective.

The same campaign's targeting of Ukrainian military and government organisations with 'Supplies for Ukraine Armed Forces' lures is Belarusian cyber support to the Russia-aligned Ukraine theatre, serving the Union State integration agenda.

Living-off-the-land espionage and persistence against Taiwanese government, education, critical-manufacturing and IT organisations is on-island intelligence collection and pre-positioning advancing the PRC's reunification objective.

Same Flax Typhoon campaign (public-disclosure framing): LOTL espionage and stealthy persistence across Taiwanese government, education, manufacturing and IT targets is on-island collection and pre-positioning serving reunification.

BlackTech's modified-firmware router implants used to pivot from subsidiaries into corporate headquarters networks (Japan/East Asia focus) is sustained corporate-technology collection consistent with Military-Civil Fusion dual-use technology acquisition; the specific sector is not named in this advisory, hence plausible.

Earth Hundun/BlackTech's Waterbear-Deuterbear espionage explicitly targets technology, research and government organisations across Asia-Pacific, mapping to Military-Civil Fusion's dual-use R&D collection from the tech and research base.

ArcaneDoor's perimeter-device implants (Line Dancer for traffic capture, Line Runner for persistence) against government networks are an espionage/collection posture on adversary government communications, loosely consistent with strategic SIGINT collection; the target set is small and unspecified, so confidence stays low.

Targeting of military and executive entities tied to China's South China Sea interests (Indonesian military, a Malaysian maritime system) timed to US-China-Indonesia naval exercises is military/maritime intelligence collection against regional C4ISR, advancing PRC strategic situational awareness in the SCS.

The campaign concentrates on 70+ Taiwanese government, academic, think-tank and technology organizations, compromising Taiwanese government bodies; this island-wide intelligence collection directly serves the reunification objective of penetrating Taiwan's state and policy apparatus.

MSS-attributed espionage against the government agencies of a NATO member (Italy) is foreign political-intelligence collection conducted under the MSS mandate; no economic or regional CN doctrine fits the generic government-agency target set, so the foundational intelligence-collection authority is the most defensible anchor.

Sustained targeting of defense, aerospace and technology organizations alongside government bodies matches the Military-Civil Fusion appetite for dual-use defense and aerospace know-how; the defense/aerospace/technology concentration is the distinguishing feature of the target set.

The op is an access-enablement technique (ISP compromise and software-update DNS poisoning to plant MACMA/MGBot implants) by a China-aligned espionage group; with no victim sector specified, the strategic WHY reduces to MSS-directed implant emplacement for collection, anchored to the intelligence-collection mandate.

CloudScout exfiltrated cloud data from a Taiwanese government entity (and a religious organization in Taiwan) via session-cookie theft; harvesting the cloud mailboxes/drives of Taiwan's state apparatus is island-side intelligence collection in service of reunification.

LIMINAL PANDA penetrates telecom networks in Southern Asia and Africa to retrieve subscriber data and call records; this communications-intelligence collection across the Belt-and-Road partner-country footprint advances political-intelligence gathering on those states' populations and elites.

CL-STA-0048 targeted a South Asian telecommunications organization and government employees to steal personal and sensitive data; theft of government-employee PII and telecom data in a Belt-and-Road partner region is partner-country political-intelligence collection.

Lotus Blossom's Sagerunex espionage hits government, telecommunications, manufacturing and media across Southeast Asia (Philippines, Vietnam, Hong Kong, Taiwan); the regional neighbor / South-China-Sea-claimant footprint maps to political-intelligence collection on Belt-and-Road partner and periphery states.

The indictment frames i-Soon (Anxun) as a private PRC firm conducting intrusions at the direction of the MSS/MPS and selling stolen data to provincial bureaus; this hacker-for-hire-at-state-direction ecosystem is the operational embodiment of the National Intelligence Law's compelled cooperation of PRC-domiciled entities.

UNC3886 plants stealthy TINYSHELL backdoors on Juniper routers inside US and Asian defense, technology and telecommunications networks, defeating Veriexec for durable covert persistence; deep, persistent access to defense and telecom routing fabric is strategic SIGINT collection against adversary command-and-communications infrastructure.

ESET assesses FishMonger as operated by the contractor I-SOON (corroborated by the DOJ I-SOON indictment); the global political-espionage target set (governments, NGOs, think tanks, religious bodies) executed through a state-tasked private contractor reflects the National Intelligence Law's compelled-contractor collection mandate rather than any single economic or regional doctrine.

BADBAZAAR and MOONSHINE trojanise apps to surveil Uyghurs, Tibetans, Taiwan-independence supporters, Hong Kong/China democracy advocates and Falun Gong on behalf of the Chinese state; this is textbook transnational-repression collection against diaspora, dissident and ethnic-minority communities.

APT15-linked PurpleHaze ran reconnaissance against a cybersecurity vendor (SentinelOne) and its IT/logistics supply chain plus 70+ global organizations using ShadowPad/GoReShell; targeting a security vendor and its supply chain is MSS-directed counter-intelligence/capability collection that fits no economic or regional CN doctrine, so the intelligence-collection mandate is the most defensible anchor.

APT41's TOUGHPROGRESS spearphishing spans government plus shipping/logistics, media/entertainment, technology and automotive sectors; the broad, mixed political-and-economic target set has no single dominant strategic doctrine, so MSS-directed multi-sector espionage is anchored to the intelligence-collection mandate (the suggested intelligentized-warfare fit is declined as its pillars are adversary-military-C4ISR-specific).

LapDogs is not a collection operation in itself but covert relay infrastructure (1,000+ compromised SOHO/edge devices) purpose-built to obscure and sustain China-nexus espionage. It services the persistent-collection enterprise this doctrine underpins; no single pillar (system-destruction, pre-positioning, SIGINT) cleanly fits obfuscation infrastructure, hence null.

HOUKEN/UNC5174 operates as an initial-access broker feeding broad foreign intelligence collection — here against a NATO state's government, telecom, media, finance and transport sectors. This is the wide, vendor-and-edge-enabled foreign collection the 2017 NIL authorizes and compels; neither named pillar (PRC-vendor cooperation, diaspora) matches the target set, so pillar is null.

Sustained, multi-actor espionage against Taiwan's semiconductor manufacturing, design and testing firms and supply chain directly serves China's indigenization of next-generation IT — semiconductors being the flagship MIC2025 next-gen-IT target; assessed motive is explicitly espionage on that sector.

Advanced semiconductors are the canonical 'chokepoint technology' the 14th FYP seeks to close; collection against Taiwan's fabs and the equipment/services supply chain advances chokepoint-closure as a distinct planning instrument alongside MIC2025.

Salt Typhoon's deep compromise of a US Army National Guard network — harvesting cross-state network diagrams, fusion-center linkages and credentials that 'could enable follow-on intrusions' against state CII — is coercive-options pre-positioning against the US homeland to degrade/delay the US response in a Taiwan contingency.

The same access — administrator credentials, configuration files and traffic diagrams enabling follow-on intrusions into state-level critical-infrastructure defense — is textbook pre-positioning in adversary critical infrastructure as an operational warfighting doctrine, distinct from the political reunification objective.

GENESIS PANDA is a high-volume cloud-conscious initial-access broker running broad-spectrum operations across financial, media, telecom and tech sectors in 11+ countries — enabling the wide foreign intelligence collection the 2017 NIL authorizes. Target breadth fits no single named pillar, so pillar is null.

Patient, long-term intrusions into telecom providers specifically to harvest call-detail records and communications telemetry are strategic SIGINT collection on communications infrastructure — the core of this doctrine's SIGINT/C4ISR-collection pillar.

UNC6384 (Mustang Panda) targeting diplomats across Southeast Asia — a core BRI partner region — via captive-portal AitM to deliver PlugX/SOGU is diplomatic political-intelligence collection on BRI partner-country governments, consistent with the actor's long-standing SE Asian diplomatic beat.

MURKY PANDA's trusted-relationship SaaS/cloud-provider compromises to reach downstream government, technology, academic, legal and professional-services victims for email espionage in North America constitute broad foreign political/economic collection under the 2017 NIL; the multi-sector target set matches no single named pillar, so pillar is null.

TA415's spearphishing of US government, think-tank and academic targets with US-China economic-relations lures, explicitly to collect intelligence on US trade policy and economic negotiations, directly serves the external-circulation goal of understanding and exploiting external dependencies for asymmetric leverage in economic statecraft.

WARP PANDA's long-dwell BRICKSTORM/ESXi espionage against US technology and manufacturing firms (and their legal counsel) aligns with PRC intelligence-collection interests in acquiring high-tech and industrial IP to advance domestic upgrading; the cross-sector spread fits no single MIC2025 sector pillar, so pillar is null.

UAT-7290 (Red Foxtrot, overlapping PLA Unit 69010) persistently targets telecommunications infrastructure; a PLA-linked actor compromising telecom is strategic SIGINT collection on communications infrastructure, the core of this doctrine's SIGINT/C4ISR pillar.

Concentration on South Asian telecom — a core BRI partner region — also serves BRI partner-country political/strategic intelligence collection, a distinct strategic logic from the military SIGINT framing.

UNC2814/GRIDTIDE's global espionage against telecommunications providers and government organizations across 42 countries is broad strategic SIGINT/communications collection; the telecom-centric global pattern fits the SIGINT/C4ISR-collection pillar better than any single regional doctrine.

Targeting of government ministries (finance, interior, foreign affairs, trade, defense), law enforcement, border control, telecom and CII across 37+ countries resembles PRC strategic political/SIGINT collection. Marked contested because Unit 42 explicitly declines country attribution (placeholder TGR-STA / 'state-aligned, out of Asia'), so the PRC 'why' rests only on the atlas's tentative cn placement.

UAT-8302's tasking is long-term access to government entities, specifically South American and Southeastern European governments — both BRI/Digital Silk Road partner regions. Sustained political-intelligence collection on partner-country governments is the strategic logic of the BRI political-intelligence pillar.

Mandiant assesses the highly targeted, evasive hypervisor implantation as cyber-espionage against defense, technology and telecommunications targets. Stealthy persistent collection on adversary strategic infrastructure (virtualization fabric underpinning C4ISR-relevant networks) maps to intelligentized-warfare's strategic SIGINT/C4ISR collection.

UNC3886 burned a FortiOS zero-day to implant network-edge security appliances (FortiGate/FortiManager/FortiAnalyzer) for persistent traffic redirection and access — the same China-nexus defense/telecom espionage cluster. Covert collection access on adversary network-edge C4ISR infrastructure maps to intelligentized-warfare's strategic SIGINT/C4ISR collection pillar.

Mandiant describes UNC3886 as a Chinese cyber-espionage group primarily targeting defense, technology and telecommunications, here using an ESXi zero-day for stealthy privileged guest access and segmentation bypass. Persistent covert collection across adversary strategic infrastructure maps to intelligentized-warfare's strategic SIGINT/C4ISR collection.

The campaign explicitly targets Tibetans (a regime-sensitive ethnic-minority/diaspora population) via a Tibetan-festival watering hole and trojanized Tibetan-language software, with victims across India, Taiwan, Hong Kong, Australia and the US. Surveillance of an ethnic-minority diaspora is the diaspora/dissident/ethnic-minority collection pillar.

Symantec attributes the retooled Daggerfly espionage to targeting organizations in Taiwan and a US-based NGO operating inside mainland China, which Symantec characterizes as internal espionage. Surveillance of a foreign NGO inside China and Taiwan-based entities maps to dissident/diaspora and internal-security collection.

This Evasive Panda/Daggerfly DNS-poisoning MgBot campaign is the same actor whose tasking centers on Tibetan, Uyghur and diaspora/dissident surveillance; detected victims in Turkey, China and India fit that internal-security and diaspora-collection mission. The summary describes method (DNS poisoning, fake updaters) rather than the strategic target set, so the doctrinal fit is inferred from actor pattern, not stated.

VIGINUM/SGDSN publicly assessed the Storm-1516 information-manipulation operation as Russia-linked, paired with the Foreign Affairs ministry condemning Russia's destabilizing activities. Public strategic attribution of a foreign influence operation is the explicit purpose of the LII strategic-attribution pillar.

Duqu 2.0 targeted the venues and parties of the P5+1 nuclear negotiations with Iran. Collection on the diplomatic track governing Iran's nuclear program directly serves Israel's counter-Iran-nuclear-program intelligence objective.

Israel compromised Kaspersky Lab to observe Russian exploitation of its telemetry and harvest intelligence on NSA tooling — a technical-collection penetration of a third-party security vendor rather than a counter-Iran or counter-Resistance action. It maps best (if loosely) to QME's technological-collection-supremacy pillar.

The summary states Israel launched the Shahid Rajaee Port disruption in retaliation for Iran's attempted poisoning of Israeli water/sewage facilities — an explicit tit-for-tat cyber strike against Iran, which is precisely the retaliatory-cyber-strike pillar.

SideWinder is the index actor for India's regional strategic-collection doctrine, and this campaign continues its government/military targeting while expanding to nuclear-energy agencies and maritime/port infrastructure across South Asia, the Middle East and Africa. The doctrine is the right umbrella, but the nuclear/maritime/Africa expansion exceeds the three named pillars (Pakistan-military, China, diplomatic), so pillar is left null.

An India-aligned actor striking Pakistani telecom critical infrastructure (5G/satcom/DevOps engineers) and reusing Pakistani Counter-Terrorism-Department credentials during Operation Sindoor is conflict-timed intelligence collection against the Pakistani state, the core of India's regional-collection mission.

Proofpoint/Threatray assess with high confidence that Bitter gathers intelligence for the Indian state against government and diplomatic entities in China, Pakistan and other neighbours plus Bangladeshi military/tax documents — the regional diplomatic/government collection that defines this doctrine.

DoNot Team is an India-suspected regional-collection actor and this foreign-ministry espionage advances the same state diplomatic-intelligence mission, but the target is a Southern European ministry — explicitly an expansion beyond the doctrine's named South Asian scope — so no regional pillar applies.

Confucius is an India-aligned actor whose sustained phishing waves against Pakistan exfiltrate documents, screenshots and Chrome/WhatsApp credentials — classic India-on-Pakistan intelligence collection consistent with this doctrine's Pakistan-targeting pillar.

Mysterious Elephant targets government and foreign-affairs organisations across Pakistan, Bangladesh, Afghanistan, Nepal and Sri Lanka, stealing documents and WhatsApp data — regional diplomatic/government collection; India-nexus rests on tool-sharing with Bitter/Confucius and is itself only plausible.

Confucius bespoke backdoors targeting military personnel (and businessmen) across South Asia and exfiltrating files from ~60 victims is the India-aligned regional military-intelligence collection at the centre of this doctrine.

Hornbill/SunBird Android surveillanceware against Pakistani military personnel and a Pakistan Atomic Energy Commission applicant — exfiltrating messages, geolocation and files — is squarely India-on-Pakistan military and nuclear intelligence collection.

A Confucius campaign spoofing the Pakistani Armed Forces' ISPR wing and using Pegasus-anxiety lures to deliver a file stealer to Pakistani military personnel is direct India-aligned collection against the Pakistani military.

Bitter spear-phishing senior officials of Bangladesh's Rapid Action Battalion — requesting call-data records and case lists — is regional government/counterterrorism collection against a neighbouring security service, matching the diplomatic/counterterrorism collection pillar.

Bitter spear-phishing a Chinese government agency to implant a Havoc trojan for remote control and data theft is direct India-aligned collection against the PRC state, matching the China-collection pillar.

Bitter's espionage RATs (WmRAT/MiyaRAT) against a Turkish defence-sector organisation advance the actor's state intelligence-collection mission, but Turkey falls outside this doctrine's named South Asian scope and no Pakistan/China/diplomatic pillar applies.

Secureworks links the Abraham's Ax and Moses Staff hacktivist personas to the Iranian COBALT SAPLING group that publishes stolen data — exactly the IRGC/MOIS practice of laundering state operations through deniable named personas that this doctrine describes.

The same cluster leaks data from Israeli companies and doxxes individuals tied to Unit 8200 (and hits Saudi ministries), advancing the Axis of Resistance's coordinated anti-Israel and anti-Saudi coercive posture.

Peach Sandstorm (APT33), an IRGC-nexus actor, runs broad password-spray access operations and cloud reconnaissance for sustained collection against satellite, defence and pharmaceutical sectors — the IRGC's mandate-driven parallel intelligence-collection apparatus rather than a coercive or retaliatory operation.

Agrius steals PII/IP and then deploys novel wipers to brick endpoints at Israeli higher-education and technology organisations — destructive, coercive anti-Israel action that is the operational expression of the Axis-of-Resistance posture.

IRGC-nexus watering-hole operations profiling and implanting Israeli transportation, maritime, logistics and tech organizations in October 2023 are coordinated anti-Israel cyber pressure timed to the Gaza war, the core coercive logic of Iran's Axis-of-Resistance posture against Israel.

Long-dwell MOIS espionage against government, military, telecom, financial and IT targets spread across Saudi Arabia, the UAE, Jordan, Kuwait, Oman, Iraq and Israel is regional intelligence collection across Iran's forward theater, consistent with fighting/collecting at maximum distance rather than a single anti-Israel coercive campaign.

IRGC-suspected collection against the aerospace and defense sector via a fake aerospace HR/job portal is intelligence collection on adversary military-industrial capability, supporting the forward-defense logic of contesting militarily superior adversaries through asymmetric means at distance.

Reaching Stage 2 of the ICS kill chain by compromising PLCs and deploying custom backdoors on OT devices across US, Australian, UK and Israeli victims is direct positioning against civilian critical infrastructure, the asymmetric coercive lever Iran wields against stronger adversaries.

Dragos assesses substantial technical overlap with the pro-Iranian hacktivist persona CyberAv3ngers, indicating the OT activity is laundered through a deniable hacktivist front in line with Iran's persona-laundering doctrine.

Void Manticore is an explicitly MOIS-affiliated actor running its destructive wiper operations behind the 'Karma' (Israel) and 'Homeland Justice' (Albania) hacktivist personas with claim-and-leak, the textbook laundering of a state destructive operation through a deniable persona.

The BiBi wiper (named for PM Netanyahu) deployed in data-theft-wipe-leak operations claiming 40+ Israeli victims is a destructive anti-Israel coercive campaign at the heart of the Axis-of-Resistance posture.

MOIS-attributed MuddyWater escalated sharply after October 2023 and concentrated its BugSleep spear-phishing on Israeli municipalities, airlines, travel agencies and journalists, an anti-Israel intelligence push aligned with the wartime coercive logic of the Resistance Axis; broader regional targets fit the same posture.

IRGC-attributed espionage with the Tickler backdoor against satellite, communications, oil-and-gas and government targets in the US and UAE is intelligence collection against the principal adversary (US) and a regional rival, the forward-defense logic of contesting stronger adversaries at distance.

A multi-year intrusion into a Middle Eastern critical-national-infrastructure entity with OT reconnaissance and credential harvesting but little exfiltration, explicitly assessed as pre-positioning for a future destructive attack, is the civilian-CII coercion lever of Iran's asymmetric-warfare doctrine.

Persistent OilRig-subgroup espionage against Kurdistan Regional Government diplomats, high-ranking Iraqi officials and a regional telecom is political/diplomatic intelligence collection across Iran's immediate periphery, the forward theater where the forward-defense posture concentrates collection.

An IRGC-nexus fake veterans' job site delivering reconnaissance and RAT malware to US military veterans is collection against the principal adversary's military community, consistent with the forward-defense logic of contesting the US through asymmetric intelligence operations.

Supply-chain compromise of Saudi Arabian IT providers to reach their downstream customers is intelligence collection against a primary regional rival, the kind of forward, distance-collection operation the forward-defense posture drives; the regional rival framing also sits within the Axis-of-Resistance's anti-Saudi orientation.

An Iran-affiliated actor conducting espionage then destructive wiper attacks (disguised as ransomware) against Israeli targets and a UAE nation-owned facility is a destructive anti-Israel coercive campaign, the core of the Resistance-Axis posture; the ransomware disguise also reflects the doctrine's preference for deniable destructive effect.

A years-long IRGC-aligned social-engineering operation ('Marcella Flores') culminating in LEMPO malware against an employee of a US aerospace defense contractor is targeted collection against the principal adversary's defense-industrial base, the forward-defense logic of contesting the US asymmetrically.

A suspected Iranian actor deploying its custom Apostle ransomware against an Israeli higher-education institution continues a pattern of destructive, ransomware-styled operations against Israeli targets, an anti-Israel coercive act consistent with the Axis-of-Resistance posture.

A purely destructive wiper (no ransomware cover) delivered via supply chain, with primary impact on Israeli victims, is coercive destructive cyber whose strategic logic is the Axis-of-Resistance anti-Israel posture rather than any revenue motive.

APT37's targeting of South Korean users, North Korean defectors, policymakers, journalists and human-rights figures is classic political-intelligence and internal-security collection, the core of the strategic-information / defection doctrine.

FakePenny ransomware demanding $6.6M in BTC, alongside fake companies and IT-worker schemes blending espionage with revenue generation, is hard-currency aggregation outside the legitimate financial order — the Office 39 sanctions-evasion principle.

Coordinated Lazarus/Kimsuky/Andariel theft of confidential technical data from 83 South Korean defense companies maps directly to strategic technology collection feeding the 8th Congress 13-system weapons-development plan.

BlueNoroff social-engineering of crypto/DeFi employees to steal company cryptocurrency is direct hard-currency generation outside the legitimate financial order, the operative principle of the Office 39 sanctions-evasion doctrine.

The summary explicitly states the group focuses on consistent cryptocurrency theft to generate revenue for the regime; diverting the fintech victim's crypto to adversary wallets is direct hard-currency aggregation under the Office 39 principle.
Ruby Sleet's compromise of aerospace, defense, satellite and weapons-systems organizations is strategic technology collection in direct service of the 8th Congress 13-system weapons-development plan.

The summary states Sapphire Sleet conducted cryptocurrency-theft campaigns netting more than $10M; VC- and recruiter-impersonation to deliver crypto-stealing malware is direct hard-currency generation under the Office 39 sanctions-evasion principle.

A multi-year fraudulent remote-IT-worker scheme using forged identities and laptop farms to earn wages laundered through a Chinese bank account is precisely the global laundering / front network that aggregates DPRK hard currency under Office 39.
TA427/Kimsuky targeting of think tanks working on North Korean affairs to deliver QuasarRAT is adversary policy-planning collection, the core objective of the strategic-information doctrine.

Spear-phishing of foreign (mostly European) embassies in Seoul with fake meeting invites to deploy XenoRAT for keylogging and surveillance is diplomatic policy-collection, the core of the strategic-information doctrine.

Operation DreamJob targeting European defense firms heavily involved in the UAV/drone sector (including producers of UAVs deployed in Ukraine) is strategic weapons-technology collection feeding the 8th Congress 13-system plan.

APT37 spear-phishing of South Korean intelligence-research-association academics and former government officials is adversary policy-planning and political-intelligence collection, the core of the strategic-information doctrine.

GTIG describes UNC1069 (overlapping BlueNoroff) as a financially motivated North Korea-nexus actor; a mass-reach axios npm supply-chain compromise deploying cross-platform backdoors is financially driven revenue generation under the Office 39 sanctions-evasion principle.

A ~$292M crypto theft attributed to Lazarus/TraderTraitor with on-chain pre-funding and laundering is hard-currency generation outside the legitimate financial order — the core Office 39 sanctions-evasion logic; the documented laundering chain maps directly to the global laundering-network pillar.

Trojanizing a Yanbian gaming platform to surveil ethnic Koreans 'deemed of interest to the North Korean regime' is classic political-intelligence / internal-security collection against a population of regime concern, not revenue generation — squarely the strategic-information and political-defection doctrine.

A South-Korea-linked actor burning five zero-days against North Koreans and North-Korea-focused professionals is collection directed at the ROK's principal adversary, advancing the standing NIS counter-DPRK cyber posture; no listed pillar matches offensive collection so pillar is null.

Inexsmar's 'Pyongyang Directory Group' lure targeting political figures working on North Korea points to a South-Korea-linked actor collecting on DPRK-related policy targets, consistent with the NIS counter-DPRK posture; pillar null as no listed pillar covers offensive political-intelligence collection.

CapraRAT Android spyware delivered via consumer-app lures while sustaining focus on individuals in the Indian government/military space is collection against Indian defense personnel — directly the Pakistan-aligned asymmetric posture's military-collection pillar.

Harvesting NIC portal credentials and Kavach 2FA codes from Indian defense personnel to enable long-term infiltration of defense networks is textbook Indian-military collection under Pakistan's asymmetric cyber posture against India.

DeskRAT phishing themed on Indian defence matters and specifically tailored to India's BOSS Linux distribution used in military and government networks is collection against Indian military systems — the military-collection pillar of the asymmetric posture against India.

Cross-platform Geta/Ares/DeskRAT campaigns by APT36 and SideCopy against Indian defense and government-aligned organizations for data theft and persistent access advance the Indian-military-collection pillar of Pakistan's asymmetric posture against India.

A GRU-associated actor linked to the WhisperGate destructive MBR-wiper used against Ukraine is conducting destructive operations in the priority Ukrainian theater that the Russkiy Mir frame designates as Russia's continuous cyber theater.

Zero-day webmail espionage against European governmental entities and a think tank is collection against NATO-member states, consistent with the doctrine's framing of NATO as the principal external threat and the locus of priority intelligence collection.

Roundcube zero-day espionage harvesting webmail from European governmental entities targets NATO-member states, aligning with the doctrine's NATO-as-principal-threat framing that prioritizes collection against the alliance.

FSB-run Gamaredon's sustained, high-tempo spearphishing of Ukrainian governmental institutions through 2024 is the archetypal expression of Ukraine as the continuous priority cyber theater under the Russian-World frame; the FSB's persistent collection against Kyiv organs operationalizes the near-abroad doctrine.

Turla's bespoke backdoor was deployed specifically against a Polish NGO working to support Ukraine during the invasion, mapping directly to the post-2022 posture of intelligence collection against entities and states facilitating Western aid to Ukraine.

GOLD BEGONIA is a financially motivated Russia-based RaaS crew with no stated state tasking; absent a clearer strategic fit, the tolerated criminal-proxy ecosystem (whose ransomware revenue and disruptive effect Russia permits as a strategic asset post-2022) is the only doctrine that reasonably frames it.

GRU Unit 26165's GooseEgg credential-theft/lateral-movement tooling targets Ukrainian plus Western European and North American government, NGO, and transport bodies — GRU collection against NATO-state institutions enacts the 2014 doctrine's framing of NATO as the principal external military danger.

Storm-1811 is a financially motivated Russia-nexus crew deploying Black Basta ransomware via vishing; with no explicit state objective, the tolerated criminal-proxy lane — disruptive ransomware crews Russia permits to operate post-2022 — is the closest doctrinal frame.

The SVR's sustained Diplomatic Orbiter espionage against the French MFA, Ministry of Culture, ANCT and French embassies (incl. Kyiv) is classic foreign-policy collection against a leading NATO/EU state, advancing the doctrine's NATO-as-principal-threat framing through diplomatic intelligence on Western Ukraine policy.

GRU Unit 29155's sabotage-and-espionage campaign against US and global critical infrastructure — jointly called out by Ukraine and frontline NATO states (Czechia, Estonia, Germany, Latvia) — targets the Western support infrastructure backing Ukraine, the interdiction logic at the core of the December-2021 NATO posture.

The leaked chats confirm Black Basta as a prolific Russian-language ransomware operation with Moscow offices and a tacit expectation of protection by Russian authorities — directly evidencing the tolerated/mobilizable criminal-proxy ecosystem the post-2022 posture relies on.

UNC4221's Signal phishing kit mimics Kropyva, the artillery-guidance app of the Ukrainian armed forces, to hijack soldiers' accounts and geolocate them — battlefield-focused collection against Ukrainian military personnel, the continuous-priority-theater logic of the Russian-World frame.

KAMACITE (SANDWORM-overlapping, ELECTRUM access enabler) systematically mapped entire US industrial control loops — HMIs, variable-frequency drives, metering modules, cellular gateways — which is OT/ICS pre-positioning for potential disruptive grid/utility effect, the operational core of energy weaponization.

GRU Unit 26165's 2021-2024 targeting of ~10 French entities — public services, defence, aerospace, finance, and a Paris-2024 Olympics body — is GRU collection against a core NATO state's government and defense-industrial base, enacting the doctrine's NATO-as-principal-threat framing.

FSB-linked COLDRIVER's LOSTKEYS selectively targets current/former advisors to Western governments and militaries, think tanks, journalists, NGOs and Ukraine-connected individuals — FSB document-theft against the Western policy/military establishment, advancing the NATO-as-principal-threat framing.

The Russia-based DanaBot MaaS botnet ran a dedicated second variant targeting military, diplomatic and law-enforcement personnel across North America and Europe and exfiltrating to separate state-aligned infrastructure — the textbook fusion of criminal proxy and state intelligence tasking the post-2022 posture mobilizes.

Play/Playcrypt is a prolific Russia-nexus double-extortion ransomware operation (~900 victims) with no stated state objective; absent a clearer fit, the tolerated criminal-proxy ecosystem Russia permits to operate post-2022 is the closest doctrinal frame.

FSB Center 16 leverages state control of the domestic ISP/telecom layer to run AitM surveillance of foreign diplomats in Moscow, treating the domestic information sphere as a national-security collection domain. The operation operationalizes the 2016 doctrine's framing of the information sphere as a controlled state domain to be exploited for intelligence on foreign presence.

Akira is a Russia-tolerated criminal ransomware enterprise (possible Conti lineage, Storm-1567 cluster) whose Western-CII intrusions are assessed an imminent threat to critical infrastructure, consistent with the post-2022 posture of permitting/benefiting from criminal proxies that degrade adversary CII. Link is contested because the operation is fundamentally revenue-driven rather than state-directed.

GRU/Sandworm-linked ELECTRUM executed the first coordinated OT attack on distributed energy resources (wind/solar) on the Polish grid, the direct operational expression of treating electric-grid availability as a coercive political instrument. Lineage to the 2015 Ukraine outage and Industroyer reinforces the grid-disruption doctrine.

Poland is a frontline NATO/EU state and the principal logistics hub for Western aid to Ukraine; an OT-scale strike on its energy infrastructure fits the post-2022 targeting of Ukraine-aiding states' critical infrastructure.

The campaign spoofs real European security/defense events to phish credentials from individuals working on foreign-policy/European matters and former senior US officials — strategic intelligence collection squarely against the NATO/Western policy community that the doctrine frames as the principal external threat.

FSB Center 18 (Gamaredon) espionage against Ukrainian military, security, and government targets, focused on files suggesting sensitive military information that could support Russian kinetic operations — collection in the continuous Ukraine priority theater that defines the Russkiy Mir frame.

Gamaredon used USB-borne LNK loaders and an upgraded GammaSteel stealer to exfiltrate sensitive military files from a Western country's military mission based in Ukraine — espionage prosecuted within the Ukraine theater that is the core Russkiy Mir collection priority.

Two FSB-linked groups combined access so Turla could run its Kazuar backdoor on the most valuable Ukrainian government and defense machines — high-value strategic collection concentrated in the Ukraine priority theater of the Russkiy Mir frame.

APT32/OceanLotus deployed the PHOREAL backdoor against a domestic Vietnamese financial-services institution, consistent with state-aligned collection on strategic economic sectors that supports Vietnam's industrial/economic upgrading posture. Pillar left null — the financial sector matches neither the automotive nor aerospace sub-pillars.

APT32 targeted a large Vietnamese agribusiness of national food-production importance with bespoke SPECTRALVIPER tooling — domestic strategic-sector collection in service of industrial/economic upgrading. Pillar left null — agribusiness/food production matches neither the automotive nor aerospace sub-pillars.

A multi-year APT32 intrusion of a non-profit supporting Vietnamese human rights is domestic regime-security surveillance of a civil-society/dissident target, the operational core of Resolution 29's regime-stability surveillance task.
